requirementsMet: Public-sector / SLED selling — Knute has dedicated public-sector AE roles at Adobe, Salesforce, DocuSign, and BDNA; deep familiarity with state, local, and federal civilian buying motions.
Salesforce CRM — Knute is a former Salesforce employee; uses SFDC natively for pipeline management.
Executive presence — Knute regularly engages CIOs, CISOs, and agency leadership; routinely the senior voice in the room on six- and seven-figure deals.
AI / applied-AI narrative — Co-Founder of TrackLight.ai, building AI investigative analytics for government; credible AI storyteller.
Quota over-attainment — Averaged ~130% of quota over four years at Adobe Public Sector; consistent over-performer.
Large-deal management — Closed $2M+ ACV at DocuSign and multiple seven-figure deals at Adobe and Salesforce.
Pipeline generation — Built and sustained multi-million pipelines across multiple enterprise roles; comfortable with outbound and partner-led motions.
Channel / partner motion — Worked with Carahsoft, the SI ecosystem, and federal-focused partners across Adobe, Salesforce, and DocuSign.
requirementsNotMet: Specific product-line fluency — Knute will need a short ramp on the company's particular platform, although his sales motion transfers cleanly.

jobTitle: Account Executive - Public Safety Software
rawText:
Account Executive – Public Safety Software

Confidential Client – Public Sector Technology

Search Managed by Peskind Executive Search




Job Type

Full-Time Regular

Location

Remote — WESTERN U.S.




Overview

Our client is seeking a consultative Account Executive to drive growth within public safety and government agencies across a defined U.S. territory. This is an opportunity to join an established and growing software provider delivering mission-critical solutions to law enforcement and public sector organizations.




This role offers a strong mix of net-new business development and expansion opportunities within existing accounts. The ideal candidate is comfortable managing complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles and selling into public sector organizations.




Key Responsibilities

Own and execute territory strategy within assigned region
Identify and develop net-new public safety and government agency relationships
Drive expansion and multi-product adoption within existing customers
Manage full-cycle sales from discovery through contract execution
Lead product demonstrations, proposals, and negotiations
Navigate complex public sector procurement and RFP processes
Build trusted relationships with public safety leadership, IT, and procurement stakeholders
Maintain accurate pipeline management and forecasting within CRM
Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, product, and customer success teams
Attend industry events and conferences to generate pipeline and build relationships




Ideal Background

5+ years of SaaS or technology sales experience
Experience selling into public sector, government, or public safety organizations preferred
Track record of managing complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles
Strong consultative selling and value-based sales approach
Experience with long sales cycles and structured procurement processes
Ability to independently generate and manage pipeline
Comfortable working in a growing, entrepreneurial environment




Preferred Experience

Public safety software sales (CAD, RMS, Dispatch, Compliance, or related solutions)
Experience selling to law enforcement, corrections, municipalities, or state agencies
Familiarity with RFP-driven public sector buying processes
Background working with cloud-based or enterprise SaaS platforms




Requirements

Remote within the United States
Willingness to travel for client meetings and industry events as needed
Strong executive presence and relationship-building skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
… more

Featured benefits

Medical insurance, Vision insurance, Dental insurance, 401(k), Paid paternity leave, Paid maternity leave

Requirements added by the job poster

• 3+ years of work experience with Software as a Service (SaaS)

jobTitle: Senior Sales Executive - Public Sector
notes: Hybrid in Atlanta — friction from Apollo Beach FL. 4 UMD alumni at Worldpay/Global Payments per LinkedIn.
rawText:
Senior Sales Executive - Public Sector
Worldpay (now part of Global Payments) — Atlanta, GA · Hybrid · Full-time

Job Description
Are you ready to write your next chapter? Make your mark at one of the biggest names in payments. We're looking for a Senior Sales Executive to join our ever-evolving Enterprise Public Sector team and help us unleash the potential of every business.

What You'll Own
As a Sr. Sales Executive, Public Sector, you will be charged with developing and managing an identified market segment that is strategic to Worldpay's long-term growth objectives. You will be involved in identifying new target segments, collecting information that would be of assistance in developing product solutions, and understanding the revenue possibilities available to Worldpay on an annual basis, forming partnerships with key influencers in your segment.

- Responsible for new account development
- Closing business with new accounts
- As an expert in the field, uses professional concepts in developing resolution to critical issues and broad design matters.
- Identify new client targets and key contact people within public sector, loading into Salesforce.com
- Consistently achieve quota and proactively manage opportunities to maintain appropriate mix of prospects at various stages of the sales cycle
- Sell to C-level executives within target segments

What You'll Bring
- As an ideal candidate you will have established relationships in the public sector vertical (state, local or federal government) and strong knowledge of payments processing
- Must have 7+ years of experience selling to financial decision makers within the Public Sector (CFO, Controller, Finance Manager, Director of Finance)
- You will have proven ability to sell business technology solutions to large enterprise companies
- Excellent presentation and writing skills
- Ability to thrive in a high-performance team
- Ability to travel up to 50%

Compensation
For this full-time position, the good faith estimated annual salary range upon hire is $118,300 - $175,975 base. Actual starting salary will be determined by experience, job-related skills, and relevant education or training.

About Worldpay
Worldpay combined with Global Payments in January 2026 to create a leading payments technology and software company that powers commerce for businesses of all sizes worldwide. Global Payments processes billions of transactions and trillions in annual payment volume across more than 175 countries. Headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, Global Payments is a Fortune 500 company and a member of the S&P 500.

Note: 4 University of Maryland alumni have been hired at Worldpay (per LinkedIn).

requirementsMet: 7+ years selling to public sector financial decision-makers — 25+ years enterprise tech sales with dedicated Public Sector roles at Adobe, Salesforce, BDNA reaching CFO / Controller / Finance Director personas in state and local government.

Established public sector relationships — Career-long network across state, local, federal civilian agencies through Adobe Public Sector, Salesforce Public Sector, BDNA State & Local Government leadership.
Selling business technology to large enterprise — Closed $2M+ ACV at DocuSign; 130% quota at Adobe Public Sector across four fiscal years.
C-level executive selling — Career-long pattern engaging state CIOs, agency CFOs, and program executives.
Salesforce CRM proficiency — Held Strategic Account Executive role at Salesforce itself; SFDC is a core capability.
Excellent presentation and writing — Track record of executive-level proposals and capture narratives.
Travel up to 50% — Comfortable with regional travel cadence from prior enterprise AE tenure.
University of Maryland alum — 4 UMD alumni already at Worldpay/Global Payments (cited on the posting).

Would need to ramp on Worldpay/Global Payments product line and SLED payments narratives (collections, treasury, fines/fees).
Atlanta hybrid in-office requirement — Posting is Atlanta hybrid; Knute is in Apollo Beach FL. ~7-hr drive or weekly flights, which may not match Worldpay's expectation.

Established CFO / Controller / Finance Director relationships specifically — Knute's strongest public sector personas are CIO/CTO/CISO/agency leadership; CFO-finance personas are adjacent but not the primary track.

jobTitle: Account Executive - Southeast SLED
notes: Recruiter-posted role (Glocomms) for a confidential identity-verification / fraud-prevention SaaS client. Southeast SLED is squarely Knute's geography and domain.
rawText:
Account Executive - Southeast SLED
Glocomms (recruiter — confidential client) — Texas, United States · Remote · Full-time

About Our Client
Our client is the leading platform for digital identity verification and fraud prevention, leveraging AI and machine learning to help organizations confidently establish trust online. Their solutions are trusted by some of the largest public and private institutions to combat fraud, improve access, and streamline digital experiences.

Role Overview
Our client is seeking a seasoned SLED Account Executive to drive revenue growth across State, Local, and Education (SLED) customers in the Southeast region. This role owns the full enterprise sales lifecycle, selling a mission-critical SaaS platform into complex government environments.

The ideal candidate has deep experience closing large, 7-figure enterprise deals, is comfortable navigating public sector procurement, and brings a consultative, outcomes-focused approach. Prior experience selling identity verification or fraud prevention solutions is preferred but not required. Experience selling enterprise SaaS or cybersecurity solutions is required.

Key Responsibilities
- Own and execute a comprehensive territory plan for SLED accounts across the Southeast
- Build, develop, and manage a robust pipeline of qualified public sector opportunities
- Navigate complex, multi-stakeholder sales environments including IT, security, procurement, and executive leadership
- Lead full-cycle enterprise sales motions, including RFPs, contract negotiations, and multi-year agreements
- Successfully close large, complex deals, including transactions exceeding seven figures in total contract value
- Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success, Marketing, and Product teams to deliver value-based solutions
- Develop trusted, long-term relationships with key decision makers and influencers
- Maintain accurate pipeline management, forecasting, and CRM hygiene
- Represent our client at industry events, conferences, and customer engagements

Required Qualifications
- 8+ years of enterprise sales experience, including significant exposure to SLED or public sector customers
- Demonstrated success closing 7-figure, complex SaaS or cybersecurity deals
- Experience managing long, consultative sales cycles with multiple stakeholders
- Strong understanding of government procurement processes, including RFPs, RFIs, and contracting
- Track record of consistently meeting or exceeding quota in high-value territories
- Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills at the executive level
- Willingness to travel as needed throughout the Southeast region

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience selling identity, fraud prevention, risk, or security-related solutions
- Established relationships within state, local, or education agencies in the Southeast
- Familiarity with modern cloud architectures and enterprise security platforms

Compensation: $150,000 - $175,000 base salary (OTE not disclosed). No visa sponsorship.

About Glocomms
Glocomms is a specialist technology recruitment firm founded in 2013. This posting is on behalf of a confidential identity-verification / fraud-prevention SaaS client.

About Glocomms
Glocomms is a specialist technology recruitment firm founded in 2013. This posting is on behalf of a confidential identity-verification / fraud-prevention SaaS client.
requirementsMet: 8+ years enterprise sales with significant SLED / public sector exposure — 25+ years enterprise tech sales with deep SLED tenure (Director State & Local at BDNA; Strategic AE Public Sector at Adobe and Salesforce).
Closing 7-figure complex SaaS / cybersecurity deals — Closed $2M+ ACV at DocuSign; routinely managed $1M-$5M+ public sector deal cycles at Adobe, Salesforce, BDNA.
Long, consultative sales cycles with multiple stakeholders — Career-long focus on public-sector RFP-driven cycles and multi-stakeholder buying committees.
Government procurement / RFP / RFI fluency — Career-long fluency with GSA, NASPO ValuePoint, SEWP, state masters, cooperative purchasing.
Quota over-achievement in high-value territories — 130% of quota over four years at Adobe Public Sector.
Executive-level communication and negotiation — Core strength; deep CIO / CISO / agency-leadership engagement.
Southeast geography — Apollo Beach, FL squarely in the Southeast territory the role covers.

Identity / fraud / risk adjacency — TrackLight.ai (AI investigative analytics for government program integrity) directly aligns with the client's identity-verification and fraud-prevention narrative; DocuSign signer-identity experience also adjacent.
requirementsNotMet: Specific identity-verification / fraud-prevention SaaS product sales — The client is in a defined competitive set (Socure, Persona, Jumio, Veriff, Onfido). Knute has fraud/identity adjacency but no direct platform sales tenure in this exact category; would need product ramp.

Modern cloud security architecture depth — Posting prefers familiarity with cloud architectures and enterprise security platforms; Knute's strongest depth is in agreement automation, AI analytics, and document/content cloud (Adobe, DocuSign, Box-adjacent).
Confidential employer — Recruiter-posted means the actual brand, product, and team aren't visible until later in the process. Worth a screen but evaluate the company before deep investment.

The actual hiring company is described as the leading platform for digital identity verification and fraud prevention, using AI/ML to combat fraud and streamline digital experiences for large public and private institutions. Knute should ask the recruiter to disclose the client during initial screen.
